summary: Renamed the effect unit `melee/ef/ef_061D.c`/`.h` to `melee/ef/efspecial.c`/`.h` and renamed the global effect-spawn function at 0x80061D70 from `ef_80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`. The PR propagated the rename through build/config metadata, symbols, the header include, and the `efSync_Spawn` callsite. Minor formatting/include-order churn also appears in Kirby and item files, but the substantive change is naming/documentation of an already matching effect unit.
what_changed: Renamed `src/melee/ef/ef_061D.c` to `src/melee/ef/efspecial.c` and updated its self-include from `ef_061D.h` to `efspecial.h`.
Renamed `src/melee/ef/ef_061D.h` to `src/melee/ef/efspecial.h` and changed the exported declaration from `ef_80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so the section covering `.text` 0x80061D70-0x80063930 and associated data sections is named `melee/ef/efspecial.c` instead of `melee/ef/ef_061D.c`.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` so `.text:0x80061D70` is named `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` instead of `ef_80061D70`, preserving the same function size 0x1BC0.
Updated `configure.py` to build `Object(Matching, "melee/ef/efspecial.c")` in place of `melee/ef/ef_061D.c`.
Updated `src/melee/ef/efsync.c` extern declaration and dispatch call inside `efSync_Spawn` to use `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` for `gfx_id < 0x4BA`.
Applied incidental formatting/include-order edits in `ftKb_SpecialN.c`, `ithouou.c`, `itoldkuri.c`, and `itpikachuthunder.c`; the diff does not show behavioral changes there.
smart_moves: The rename was propagated across all places needed for a matched unit: source path, header path, configure object list, split metadata, symbol name, extern declaration, and callsite.
The new symbol name describes the function's role in the `efSync_Spawn` dispatch path better than the raw-address placeholder `ef_80061D70`.
The PR preserved the original address, size, and matching status while improving searchable names, making the change useful for future effect-system work without destabilizing the decomp.

The decomp.dev bot report showed the old unit becoming broken and the new unit becoming matched with the same byte counts, which is consistent with a pure rename of a matched object rather than a code-generation change.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a matched source unit, update `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, and `configure.py` together; otherwise build/decomp metadata will refer to stale unit names.
Address-placeholder function names such as `ef_80061D70` should be replaced with subsystem-scoped descriptive names once behavior is known well enough.

For dispatcher functions like `efSync_Spawn`, renaming the target function should include the extern declaration, active call, and any explanatory commented-out call forms to keep future readers aligned.

A decomp.dev report showing equal-size 'new matches' and 'broken matches' under old/new names can be interpreted as successful rename churn, not a regression, when the addresses and byte counts match.
naming_conventions: `ef_061D.c` was replaced by the lower-case module-style filename `efspecial.c` under `src/melee/ef/`.
`ef_80061D70` was replaced by `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`, using the effect subsystem prefix `efSpecial_` plus a descriptive action name.
The header name mirrors the source filename: `efspecial.h` for `efspecial.c`.
The config split name uses the same path stem as the source file: `melee/ef/efspecial.c`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The function at `.text:0x80061D70` remained size `0x1BC0`; only its symbol name changed from `ef_80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
The unit remained marked `Object(Matching, ...)` after the file rename, indicating the matching status was preserved.

The bot reported `main/melee/ef/efspecial` `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` as 100.00% matched for +7104 bytes, while the old `main/melee/ef/ef_061D` `ef_80061D70` entry dropped by the same 7104 bytes, reinforcing that this was a metadata/name migration.
No evidence in the diff excerpt indicates a new matching trick or codegen-sensitive rewrite; the core function body was not substantively changed.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported 4 new matches for `main/melee/ef/efspecial` and 4 broken matches for the old `main/melee/ef/ef_061D` unit, consistent with the unit rename.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that further document or split `efspecial.c` or `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Search for remaining raw-address effect symbols named like `ef_8*` that may be candidates for subsystem-scoped names.
Check whether `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`'s parameters beyond `gfx_id`, `HSD_GObj*`, and `va_list` were later named or typed more precisely.
